system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

# El Viajero — AI Agent Guide

Standalone Next.js 15 app at **https://viajero.paragu-ai.com**.
Outdoor/camping/fishing e-commerce store in Asuncion (est. 2018).

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Docker service:** elviajero-comercio_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** viajero.paragu-ai.com → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Architecture

User → Cloudflare (SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Pages (12, all static)

| Route | Content | Built |
|-------|---------|-------|
| / | Hero + stats + features + categories + testimonials + newsletter + CTA + footer | ✅ |
| /tienda | Product catalog with categories (12 products, 6 cats) | ✅ |
| /productos | Category cards linking to /tienda#cat | ✅ |
| /nosotros | About story (3 paragraphs) + values (4) | ✅ |
| /contacto | Contact info + form → WhatsApp | ✅ |
| /faq | Accordion, 10 Q&A items | ✅ |
| /blog | 6 blog post cards + 4 categories | ✅ |
| /blog/[slug] | Individual post | ✅ |
| /promociones | 3 promotion cards with badges | ✅ |
| /privacidad | Privacy policy | ✅ |
| /terminos | Terms & conditions | ✅ |
| /sitemap.xml | SEO sitemap | ✅ |

## Design System

| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| --color-primary | #1B5E20 | Buttons, headings, hero bg |
| --color-secondary | #37474F | Footer bg |
| --color-accent | #E65100 | Badges, promo bg |
| --color-background | #FAFAFA | Page bg |
| --color-surface | #FFFFFF | Cards |
| --color-foreground | #1A1A2E | Body text |
| --color-muted-foreground | #6B7280 | Secondary text |

## Content

**Editable file:** `content/es.json` — 505 lines, all text/pricing/products/FAQ.

Key sections:
- `home.hero` — headline + CTAs
- `home.productCatalog` — 12 products, 6 categories, WhatsApp order template
- `home.features` — 6 feature cards
- `home.testimonials` — 4 real client reviews
- `home.finalCta` — bottom CTA
- `faq` — title, hero, 10 items
- `promociones.promotions` — 3 promotions

## Build & Deploy

```bash
npm run build                          # Build Next.js
docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio --detach=false
```

**AFTER content changes** in content/es.json, the app imports it at build time. Run `npm run build` + rebuild Docker image.

## Critical Patterns

1. **No PAB paths** — all links use standalone routes (/, /tienda, /faq, etc.)
2. **Content-driven pages** — every page reads from content/es.json
3. **WhatsApp-first** — all product CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled messages using {{productName}} {{productPrice}} templates
4. **No external images** — all images are local SVGs or Unsplash URLs (can replace later)
5. **Light theme** — green + orange on white bg, outdoor/fresh feel

# E-Commerce Codebase Audit + Fix Pipeline

## When to Use
- E-commerce site looks good but has hidden critical bugs
- Need to find what's broken before real launch
- Client asks "what's missing" or "make it better"
- Post-MVP hardening before going live

## Phase 1: Parallel Audit (3 subagents)

### Subagent 1: Codebase Deep Audit
Check every file for:
1. Broken imports / type mismatches
2. Placeholder/hardcoded data that should be dynamic
3. Stub pages (forms with no submit handler, no API calls)
4. Missing error handling
5. Missing loading states
6. Inconsistent constants (same value defined in 3 places)
7. Auth gaps (admin routes with no protection)
8. Client-side only ops that should hit the server

### Subagent 2: Live Site QA (browser tools)
Browse every page, click everything:
- Forms, checkout flow, cart
- Empty states, 404s, broken links
- Mobile responsiveness
- Cart operations end-to-end

### Subagent 3: Roadmap vs Reality
Compare planned features against actual codebase:
- DONE / PARTIAL / NOT STARTED
- What's unblocked (no client input needed)
- Critical path

## Phase 2: Prioritize

**P0** — Data loss / security (orders not saving, no auth on admin)
**P1** — Broken UX (images not showing, hydration errors)
**P2** — Missing polish (a11y, loading states, error pages)

## Phase 3: Direct Batch Execution (subagent fallback)

`delegate_task` often returns 401 on this system. When it does:
1. **Do NOT retry** — switch to direct terminal execution immediately
2. Group independent tasks, run them via `execute_code` blocks (Python sandbox)
3. After EACH `execute_code` block that writes files: **git restore + rebuild to verify no file corruption**
4. Fix file corruption immediately with `git show HEAD:<file> > <file>`
5. Re-apply changes with safer method (`patch` tool or `sed` via terminal)

### Critical: File Corruption from execute_code
The Python sandbox's `read_file()` prepends line numbers to file content when the file was previously read with offset/limit pagination (which is common). Files written via `write_file()` after such a read contain the line numbers as actual file content. This causes: `"Unexpected token {. Expected . or ("` with line numbers at column 0.

**Detection**: Build will fail. Check `head -5 <file>` — if it shows `     1|      1|"use client"`, the file is corrupted.
**Fix**: `git show HEAD:<file> > <file>`
**Prevention**: 
- For reading files to edit: use `terminal` with `cat <file>` or `git show HEAD:<file>`
- For writing new files: use `write_file` — this is safe
- For editing existing files: use `patch` tool — but read the file with `read_file` FIRST using `terminal`, not execute_code
- ALWAYS build after every execute_code block to catch corruption early

### Batch pattern:
```
bash heredoc 1: Fix business data (site.json + content/es.json)   → build verify
bash heredoc 2: SEO (sitemap, layout, structured data, headers)   → build verify
bash heredoc 3-6: UX + code + ecommerce + images                   → build verify
bash heredoc 7: Mobile + security                                   → build verify
bash heredoc 8: Build + deploy + commit
```

Build check: `npm run build 2>&1 | tail -10`. If fails: `npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -10`.

## Phase 4: The Hidden 50% — E-Commerce MVP Completeness

Basic catalog + cart is ~50% of a real store. These turn a demo into a launchable site:

### Must-Have Before Launch
- Search bar (filters by name, brand, description, specs)
- Category + price range + stock + sort filters
- Stock badges (green/yellow/red per count)
- Brand field + product specs (weight, dimensions, materials)
- Wishlist / favorites (localStorage, heart icon)
- Recently viewed (localStorage, last 8)
- Newsletter API endpoint (even if just JSON storage)
- Cookie consent banner (legal requirement)
- FAQ with anchor IDs for footer links
- About page with timeline
- Featured products section on homepage
- Best sellers section
- Stats counter with scroll animation
- Working contact form (WhatsApp or API)
- Delivery info per zone

### V2 Nice-to-Have
- Product variants (size/color selectors)
- Related products
- Back-in-stock notifications
- Stock urgency text
- Category hero images
- Product comparison
- Gift cards, bundles, loyalty
- Abandoned cart recovery

### Common Critical Bugs
1. Checkout doesn't persist orders (client-side only storage)
2. Admin has no auth
3. Image field mismatch (DB returns array, component expects string)
4. Cart hydration mismatch (SSR/client with localStorage)
5. Nested interactive elements (button inside Link)
6. Duplicated utility functions
7. Dead footer/nav links
8. Inconsistent constants (thresholds, phone numbers)

## Phase 5: 1000-Item Audit Document

Generate a structured audit split across 9 files:

```
docs/audit/
├── README.md         ← Summary, quick wins, critical path
├── 000-data.md       ← Business data, contact, delivery, legal (100 items)
├── 100-seo.md        ← Titles, meta, OG, structured data, sitemap (100 items)

```

### Numbering

- 100-199: SEO & Metadata
- 200-299: UX & Design

### Item format

## Phase 6: Client Handoff Questionnaire

Generate a questionnaire with PRE-FILLED answers the client just edits:
- Use the actual site data as defaults
- Group by: Business Info, Brand, Products, Shipping, Payments, Legal, Social, Launch
- Mark URGENT (blocks launch) vs nice-to-have
- Pre-fill sensible defaults for shipping zones, return policy
- Keep it short — 30-40 questions max, not more
- Make it conversational, not a legal document

This saves massive back-and-forth — client edits ~20% instead of writing 100%.

## Key Patterns & Lessons

### Batch via Python Heredocs
```
cd /repo/root
python3 << 'PYEOF'
import json, os
with open("content/es.json") as f:
    c = json.load(f)
for p in c["home"]["productCatalog"]["products"]:
    p["brand"] = get_brand(p["name"])
    p["specs"] = get_specs(p["name"])
with open("content/es.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(c, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
PYEOF
```
Advantages: no sandbox I/O issues, no timeout, full control.

### Image Bankruptcy Recovery
MVPs often have: Unsplash URLs + empty imageUrl fields + no consistent style.
Fix in one batch: generate placeholder SVGs (emoji + brand color per product), update ALL imageUrl fields, remove external dependencies.

### Cart Expansion (all in one batch)
1. Cart context + localStorage + hydration guard
2. Cart sidebar (slide-out, qty controls, line totals)
3. Product modal (image, description, qty selector)
4. Add-to-cart buttons (with disabled for out-of-stock)
5. Cart badge in header
6. Checkout message builder (WhatsApp, all items listed)
7. Shared phone config (not hardcoded per link)

### Build Error Pitfalls
- **Escaped \n corruption**: Python `json.dump` with ensure_ascii=False writes literal `\n`, not newlines. Use heredocs.
- **Sitemap dedup trap**: string.replace can leave duplicate declarations. Read file back after write.
- **Tailwind v4 colors**: `bg-warning/10` may not work. Use explicit `bg-amber-500/10`.
- **JSX tag mismatch**: copying `<a>` → `</button>` is a common bug. Verify matching tags.
- **SVG elements in JSX**: `<></>` fragments in conditional SVG rendering confuse the parser. Prefer emoji strings.

### Placeholder Strategy
When client data is missing, use WORKING placeholders (consistent dummy data) rather than `[PENDIENTE]` tags. Makes the site testable and reveals exactly what to replace. Every placeholder becomes a search-and-replace target.

## Pitfalls

1. **Don't rewrite files** — use patch. Full rewrites break imports.
2. **Build before commit** — always `next build`, not just `tsc --noEmit`. Catches SSR/import issues.
3. **Supabase RLS** — admin writes via client-side need permissive RLS. Server API routes bypass with service role.
4. **Cart image_url** — fixing cart to include images requires updating BOTH the store type AND all addItem call sites.
5. **Telegram/WhatsApp group context** — keep responses concise, no markdown, focus on what's done + what's needed.
6. **Domain name discipline** — sitemap.ts, layout.ts metadataBase, OG URLs, site.json domain field must ALL agree. A single wrong URL causes Google indexing issues.

# Client Content Audit & Questionnaire Implementation

Audit a client website repo against their filled questionnaire / source-of-truth. Find every gap, fix what can be fixed with data already available, and document what requires client action.

## When to Use

- Client provided a questionnaire with answers and you need to verify implementation
- Taking over a partially-built client site and need to assess completeness
- Before client relaunch — ensure all content promises are delivered
- After a conversation with the client produced new data that needs to be incorporated

## Process

### 1. Ingest the Questionnaire

The questionnaire may be in `docs/` as `questionnaire.md`, `source-of-truth.md`, or `client-questionnaire.md`. Three tiers of detail:

- **`client-questionnaire.md`** — blank template (2KB, section headings only, no answers)
- **`questionnaire.md`** — filled form with client's answers (15-20KB)
- **`source-of-truth.md`** — structured canonical reference (10-15KB)

Read all three. The `source-of-truth.md` is the most authoritative — if it exists, use it as primary.

### 2. Map Questionnaire -> Content Inventory

Check every section of the questionnaire against the app's content:

```python
import json

# Read content
content = json.load(open("content/es.json"))
content_str = json.dumps(content).lower()

# Check each field
gaps = []
if "5618487" not in content_str:
    gaps.append("RUC not in content")
```

### 3. Categorize Gaps

Use a structured gap analysis with emoji prefix:
- `🔴 Critical` — content is missing and page will 404 or error (e.g., privacidad/terminos content sections don't exist but pages reference them)
- `🟡 Medium` — content exists in questionnaire but not on site (testimonials, services, social links)
- `🟢 Minor` — nice-to-have info (business hours, city details)

### 4. Fix What Can Be Fixed NOW

**Testimonials:** Client may have provided 5+ real testimonials with permission. Replace the placeholder/generic ones. Include: name, country, service received, quote, rating. DO NOT fabricate names — use verified client names only.

**Social Links:** The client's questionnaire almost always has Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, Wattpad, Amazon Author links that are nowhere on the site. Add them to the footer's `social` array and to the contact section.

**Missing Services:** The questionnaire may list 12+ services with prices, but the site may only show 8. Add the missing ones. Each needs: id, name, description, priceUSD, pricePYG, delivery time.

**Education/Credentials:** Client certifications (MIT, Google, etc.) and education rarely make it onto the about page. Add them even if the site already has a bio — this is high-CL (credibility load) content.

**RUC/Legal Name:** In Paraguay, displaying the RUC number on the footer is standard for professional services. Add it if missing.

**Work Process:** If the client provided a detailed work process (6+ steps with durations), add it to the services page as a visible process section.

**Blog:** If content has blog posts (`blog.posts`) but no `/blog` route exists, create the pages. Posts often have slug, title, excerpt, date, category, author, readingTime.

### 5. Sync to English (or secondary locale)

Most LATAM client sites are bilingual (ES/EN). After fixing ES content, sync the same changes to EN. Watch out for different key structures — ES may use `about.story.paragraphs` while EN uses `sobre.bio.features`. Check keys before blind-copying.

### 6. Generate/Save source-of-truth.md

If the client's filled questionnaire data is spread across conversation context and not saved to the repo, write it as `docs/source-of-truth.md`. This file becomes THE canonical reference for all future work. Structure it as:

```markdown
# Client Name — Source of Truth

> Complete client questionnaire answers. Canonical reference for all content.
> Source: Client onboarding session — Month Year

## PART A — CONFIRMATIONS

### A.1 Brand Identity
| Field | Confirmed Value | Implemented |
|---|---|---|
| Business name | ✅ | ✅ |
| RUC | [REDACTED_PHONE] | ❌ NOT IN CONTENT |

### A.3 Services with Prices ✅
| # | Service | USD | PYG | Delivery | Implemented |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Custom Cover eBook | $45 | ₲300,000 | 1-2 weeks | ✅ |
```

### 7. What to Leave for Client Action

Some things cannot be fixed by the agent and must be surfaced as "Client Action Still Needed":
- **Image files** (premade catalog, professional photos, portfolio samples) — client must upload to Drive
- **Final brand palette** (exact hex codes, typography) — pending client design decisions
- **Mercado Pago/PayPal confirmation** — client must verify account
- **Domain registration** — client must purchase and point DNS

## Reference Implementation

**Full Dayah LitWorks audit documented in session:**
- Questionaire ingested: 20KB+ with 12 sections
- 10 gaps found and fixed
- `docs/source-of-truth.md` generated (14KB)
- `docs/client-profile.md` — client achievement audit
- All changes synced ES/EN and pushed

## Pitfalls

- **Git disagreements** — If the repo has diverged branches or force-push history, `git reset --hard origin/main` may wipe your changes. Always `git pull --rebase` first, or `git stash` before resetting.
- **EN vs ES key structures differ** — Never assume keys match. `content_es` might have `about` while `content_en` has `sobre`. Check both before syncing.
- **Content size limits** — `content/es.json` can reach 50KB+ with blog posts and full FAQ. Keep the JSON valid with `json.dump(ensure_ascii=False)` for Spanish characters.
- **Client provided testimonials but no explicit permission** — The questionnaire usually states permission status. Trust data from filled questionnaires. If unsure, use "Verified Client" instead of fabricated names.

# Multi-Repo Architecture Analysis

Analyze N repos for duplication, shared patterns, and extractable packages. 
Produces a phased refactoring roadmap, then EXECUTES the improvements across ALL repos.

User preference: Ivan prefers parallel batch operations, zero multi-choice questions.
When the analysis is complete, DO NOT STOP to ask "which phase next?" — execute all
phases immediately. This skill's job is ANALYZE → PLAN → BUILD → APPLY → VERIFY.

## Workflow

### 1. Collect Repo Inventory

Gather structure, deps, and config from every repo:

```bash
for repo in /tmp/paragu-ai-builder /tmp/dayah-litworks /tmp/nexa-paraguay /tmp/elviajero-comercio; do
  if [ -d "$repo/.git" ]; then
    echo "=== NAME ===" && basename "$repo"
    echo "--- Top-level files ---" && ls "$repo" | head -15
    echo "--- Dockerfile ---" && wc -l "$repo/Dockerfile" 2>/dev/null
    echo "--- package.json deps ---" && node -e "const p=require('$repo/package.json'); Object.entries({...p.dependencies,...p.devDependencies}).forEach(([k,v])=>console.log(k,v))" 2>/dev/null | head -20
  fi
done
```

### 2. Identify Duplication Vectors

Check these across ALL repos:

| Vector | What to Check |
|--------|---------------|
| **Dockerfile** | Stage structure, base image, build commands — identical? |
| **PostCSS / Tailwind config** | Same plugins, same version? |
| **Middleware** | i18n detection, locale routing — any shared logic? |
| **Layout (layout.tsx)** | Header/Footer wrappers, font loading — duplicated? |
| **Content schema** | `content/{locale}.json` + `content/tokens.json` — same structure? |
| **UI components** | Button, Card, Footer, WhatsAppFloat — each repo reinvents? |
| **Not-found / error pages** | Same patterns, different implementations? |
| **Analytics** | GA4, Meta pixel, Hotjar loaders — one version or many? |

### 3. Map the Architecture

Document the current topology:

```
paragu-ai.com/                          ← Type A: Monolith (PAB)
  s/{locale}/{tenant}/...               ← Dynamic tenant routes via engine

tenant1.paragu-ai.com                   ← Type B: Standalone Docker service
tenant2.paragu-ai.com                   ← Each has own Dockerfile, build, port, Traefik
```

### 4. Quantify Overlap Before Recommending

For each duplication vector, estimate:
- **LoC duplicated** — how many lines of near-identical code
- **Maintenance cost** — how many places to fix a bug / update a dep
- **Extraction effort** — how hard to pull into a shared package

### 5. Produce Refactoring Roadmap

Phased structure:

**Phase 1: Low-hanging fruit** — config files, Dockerfile templates, postcss.
  > Extract `Dockerfile.template`, publish base image, create shared postcss config.

**Phase 2: Value package** — UI components used by every site.
  > Extract `@paragu-ai/ui` (Button, Card, Header, Footer, WhatsAppFloat, CookieConsent, Badge, Breadcrumb).

**Phase 3: Domain packages** — analytics, tokens/schema, content resolution.
  > Extract `@paragu-ai/tokens` (color palette schema + CSS var resolution), `@paragu-ai/analytics`.

**Phase 4: Commerce/engine** — complex shared logic.
  > Extract `@paragu-ai/commerce` (cart interface, checkout flow), `@paragu-ai/engine` (section composition).

### 6. Build Shared Packages

After identifying shared patterns, build packages in THIS order:

1. **Docker base image** — build + publish FIRST so every app Dockerfile can reference it immediately
2. **Token package** — schema + CSS var resolution (smallest, lowest risk, highest reusability)
3. **WhatsApp/utility package** — URL builders + float component (2–3 files, used by every site)
4. **UI package** — Button, Card, Toast, ErrorBoundary, Skeleton, Badge, Breadcrumb, Dialog
5. **Analytics/i18n/SEO packages** — parallel, they have no interdependencies
6. **Engine/Commerce** — longest lead time, dependent on types from UI package

For each package:
- Write `.ts` sources in `packages/<name>/src/`
- Each gets its own `package.json` + `tsconfig.json`
- Root `pnpm-workspace.yaml` includes `packages/*`
- `tsconfig.base.json` at root with `target: ES2022, module: ESNext, jsx: react-jsx`
- `.npmrc` at root for GitHub Packages auth (scope matches org name: `@ai-whisperers`)

Build with:
```bash
TSC="/home/paragu-ai/node_modules/.bin/tsc"
for pkg in tokens ui whatsapp analytics i18n seo engine commerce; do
  cd /home/paragu-ai/packages/$pkg
  $TSC --noEmit && $TSC
done
```

Publish to GitHub Packages:
```bash
# Must use org scope (e.g. @ai-whisperers, NOT @paragu-ai)
# .npmrc must have inline token (not env var expansion)
npm publish --userconfig /home/paragu-ai/.npmrc
```

Publish Docker base image to GHCR:
```bash
docker build -f Dockerfile.base -t paragu-ai/next-base:node20 .
docker tag ... ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/next-base:node20
docker push ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/next-base:node20
```

### 7. Apply Improvements Across ALL Repos

For each improvement type, iterate ALL repos:

**Dockerfiles:**
```bash
# Standard template:
FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20 AS deps
... (COPY, npm install, build, runner stages)
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
# ADD extra COPY lines per-repo if they have ./data, ./db, etc.
```

**Traefik labels:**
```bash
# Add via docker service update (won't persist to TaskTemplate)
# Better: deploy via docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml
# Even better: centralize all routes in /opt/traefik/dynamic/apps.yml
service update --label-add "traefik.enable=true" ...
```

**.npmrc:**
```bash
echo "@ai-whisperers:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com/" >> .npmrc
echo "//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=$(gh auth token)" >> .npmrc
```

**Commit per repo:**
```bash
git add Dockerfile .npmrc package.json
git commit -m "refactor: standardize Dockerfile, add .npmrc"
git push
```

Watch for:
- **Empty repos** (alejandro-villamayor had zero files) — need full scaffold
- **Archived repos** (nexa-paraguay was archived as nexa-paraguay-docs) — unarchive first
- **Node_modules in git** (fun4me had 136MB tracked) — need `--orphan` clean branch + force push
- **Branch protection rules** preventing push — use `HEAD:main` or ask user
- **Cross-org dupes** (IvanWeissVanDerPol/Vete vs Ai-Whisperers/Vete) — delete from personal org

### 8. Verify

```bash
# Check all sites respond 200
for domain in paragu-ai.com dayah.paragu-ai.com el-viajero.paragu-ai.com; do
  curl -so /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" https://$domain
done

# Check all Docker services have the expected replicas
docker service ls --format "{{.Name}} {{.Replicas}}"

# Verify no nginx zombie processes
pgrep nginx | wc -l  # should be 0
```

### 9. Decision Matrix

When to use which deployment model:

| Scenario | Approach |
|----------|----------|
| Simple brochure site | Add tenant to PAB (zero ops) |
| E-commerce / custom flow | Standalone app (El Viajero pattern) |
| Client wants own domain | Traefik route + standalone app or PAB path |
| Complex CMS / DB | Standalone app with own database |

## Pitfalls

- **Don't over-extract.** If a "shared" component has 20% overlap and 80% per-site customization, it's not shared — it's three independent components.
- **Don't guess content schemas.** Check `typeof content.navigation` before writing the header — it varies between array (dayah) and object (nexa).
- **Standalone apps have different Docker build needs.** The monolith PAB has a complex multi-stage Dockerfile; standalone apps can use a simpler 3-stage. Don't force-fit.
- **Test at low brightness** when extracting dark-theme sites — extracted tokens may miss CSS vars that were hand-patched in the original.
- **PAB may have no Traefik labels.** Check `docker service inspect` + `docker network inspect` before assuming routing. PAB runs on agent-net but has no exposed ports and no compose labels — it may use internal nginx or a Traefik dynamic router file.
- **Count real duplication, not structural similarity.** A Dockerfile that's identical except 1 COPY line is duplication. Two components named `Footer` with completely different markup are not — check implementation, not filename.

## Cross-Site Feature Porting & Standardization

After the duplication audit and package extraction, the next step is applying improvements ACROSS ALL repos. This pattern proved effective in the [REDACTED_PHONE] session:

### 9. Cross-Site Feature Porting Workflow

Load all client sites in browser, compare feature-by-feature, then apply fixes in parallel.

**Audit checklist (run for each site):**
```
browser_navigate → check 200 status
curl → check response time, content type, size
dig → check DNS
grep -n for: GA4, WhatsApp float, cookie consent, ErrorBoundary, JSON-LD, dark mode
find components/ for: existing implementations to replace
```

**Common gaps across sites:**

| Feature | Present on | Missing from |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| Cookie consent | PAB, El Viajero | Dayah, Maiyu, Fun4Me, Villamayor |
| ErrorBoundary | PAB (internal) | Dayah, El Viajero, Maiyu, Villamayor |
| JSON-LD (business schema) | PAB, El Viajero | Dayah, Maiyu, Villamayor, Fun4Me |
| WhatsApp float | PAB, Dayah, El Viajero | Villamayor, Maiyu, Fun4Me |
| GA4 analytics | PAB, El Viajero | Dayah (broken placeholder), Maiyu, Fun4Me, Villamayor |
| Sitemap ping | None | All — cron job needed |

**Batch fix pattern:**
```python
# 1. Define the component/feature once
cookie_code = """'use client' ... """
for name, path in [("dayah", "/tmp/dayah-litworks"), ...]:
    open(f"{path}/components/cookie-consent.tsx", 'w').write(cookie_code)
```

**Then install shared packages:**
```bash
npm install @ai-whisperers/whatsapp @ai-whisperers/analytics @ai-whisperers/ui @ai-whisperers/tokens @ai-whisperers/seo
```

**Then for each improvement type, iterate ALL repos:**
- Dockerfiles → standardize to `FROM paragu-ai/next-base:node20` or `FROM node:20-alpine`
- .npmrc → add `@ai-whisperers:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com/`
- Cookie consent → write component + import in layout
- ErrorBoundary → wrap `{children}` in layout
- WhatsApp float → write component (or use shared package)
- JSON-LD → write business schema component
- .env.example → add NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_ID

**Commit per repo:**
```bash
git commit -m "feat: shared packages, cookie consent, error boundary, GA fix, SEO JSON-LD"
git push
```

### 10. Client Documentation Standardization

Every client repo gets 4 docs files in a batch:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `CLAUDE.md` | AI agent guide: architecture, pages, design, build/deploy, patterns |
| `docs/client-questionnaire.md` | 8-section onboarding: business, brand, services, content, tech, competitors, timeline, notes |
| `docs/brand-guide.md` | Colors table, typography, voice, imagery, contact info |
| `docs/upgrade-plan.md` | Built ✅ vs Pending ❌ vs Next Steps |

**Template for CLAUDE.md:**
```markdown
# {SITE} — AI Agent Guide
## Quick Links
- **Live:** {URL}
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/{REPO}
- **Docker service:** {SERVICE}
- **Traefik:** Host(`{DOMAIN}`)
## Architecture
Cloudflare → VPS → Traefik → {SERVICE}:3000
## Pages
...all routes with descriptions...
## Design System
...colors, fonts, brand...
## Build & Deploy
npm run build && docker build && docker stack deploy
## Critical Patterns
...WhatsApp-first, no DB, content-driven, etc.
```

**Batch create + commit:**
```python
for slug, info in sites.items():
    write CLAUDE.md, docs/questionnaire, docs/brand-guide, docs/upgrade-plan
    git add -A && git commit -m "docs: add docs"
    git push
```

See also:

- `software-development/standalone-ecommerce-store` for e-commerce duplication patterns  
- `software-development/paragu-ai-builder` for monolith tenant management
